accepted.reason: BSON decode and encode helpers over NumPy-friendly structures provide practical serialization kernels.
Matrix inversion, determinant, and rank routines form a solid numeric linear algebra example set.
Histogram and profile matching distance code gives a compact feature-engineering style numeric kernel.
Weighted means, aggregate errors, and helper statistics are reusable vectorized optimization primitives.
Straightforward Euclidean distance loops are clear baseline kernels for speedup-oriented training.
Lookup-table accumulation and benchmark-style loops map well to classic Cython optimization tasks.
2D vector arithmetic and geometry operations are compact and broadly useful math kernels.
Device-aware memoization and utility caching logic provides practical performance-pattern examples.

skipped.reason: Large project-specific query wrappers with weak standalone kernel value for GRPO unpaired set.
Legacy bot/game logic is highly domain-specific and not a good fit for general Cython speedup training.
